title: Wandergrain Supply screen-print pre-flight: audit 4 supplied traveller tee vectors for separation-readiness, registration & spot-color count, emit a marked-up seps report + print-shop go/no-go note
one_line_ask: Pre-flight four supplied traveller-themed t-shirt vector graphics for screen-print separation readiness -- verify transparent backgrounds, count distinct spot colors per design, check for registration marks and thin-line survivability, vectorize/inspect to flag raster contamination -- and emit a marked-up per-design seps audit report with a go/no-go recommendation for the exposure room.

issues: Mild softness and slight blur at skyline edges (acceptable for photoreal style, not a separation-ready spot graphic).
Mild pixelation at some edges due to tilt and transparency, but not severe.
No visible text present.
Slight motion blur/soft focus on tower spire and right-side antenna — acceptable as source input artifact
Canvas texture visible suggesting fabric substrate rather than pure digital flat graphic — minor but noted
Drop shadow present and correct
Gradient sky transitions smoothly orange to purple as specified
No hard flat color breaks — compliant
Transparent background correctly implemented
Skyline silhouette is generic/non-identifiable — compliant
Crosshair registration marks and corner crop ticks are present but rendered slightly small/subtle — may not fully read at reduced sizes

The compass rose has minor complexity that edges slightly beyond strict 3-color flat separation (some intermediate tonal blending visible in the star facets), though overall reads as clean flat spot color
Shield bottom point is partially obscured by the banner ribbon overlap, which is a minor compositional note

Only two visible text elements (no fictional brand, but not required); 'EST. 1624' is plausible as a generic detail, not a brand. The design uses a faint off-white 1px halo as described. The compass ring and crosshair appear to use a single dark tone, but the near-duplicate greys and true black are not visually distinct—hard to confirm three separate tones. No severe artifacts, misspellings, or real brands.
Faint off-white halo visible around stroke edges — acceptable per brief
Background shows checkerboard transparency pattern rather than true transparent PNG render — minor
Hairline strokes appear slightly heavier than 0.4pt spec, closer to 0.6–0.8pt at this resolution
Three near-identical dark tone requirement cannot be fully verified at JPEG compression level
Slight halo/glow effect around airplane silhouette where cream bleeds into red field — marginally soft for true hard-edge screen-print spec
Distress texture is present but subtle; could be heavier for a more convincing vintage stamp feel
Checkerboard background visible (transparent PNG rendered with alpha grid — not an asset defect, just preview artifact)
Minor inconsistency in tick-mark weight around the inner ring border — a few ticks appear slightly thicker
